name: Revolution Live
description: Fort Lauderdale's premier concert venue. Two indoor stages and an outdoor area that has hosted everyone from chart-toppers to underground heroes. If there's a show happening tonight, it's probably here.

name: Stache 1920s Drinking Den
description: A Prohibition-era speakeasy tucked in downtown Fort Lauderdale. Craft cocktails, live jazz, and DJs spinning vinyl in a dimly lit den that makes every night feel like an occasion. One of the best bars in South Florida, full stop.

name: O'Hara's Jazz Cafe
address: 722 E Las Olas Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301
description: A Las Olas institution since 1984. Live jazz seven nights a week on the sidewalk patio. Come for the music, stay for three hours longer than you planned. Old-school South Florida at its best.

name: Laser Wolf
description: Rooftop taco bar downtown with panoramic views of the city. Frozen margaritas, strong tacos, and the kind of open-air setting that makes you feel like you're exactly where you should be.

name: Shooters Waterfront
description: Right on the Intracoastal with live music on the water. Boats pull up to the dock, the sun sets behind the palms, and nobody checks their phone. A true South Florida experience.

name: Blue Martini Fort Lauderdale
address: 2432 E Sunrise Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33304
description: Upscale lounge at The Galleria with live music, DJs, and one of the best happy hours in Broward. The crowd is energetic, the cocktails are strong, and the dance floor fills up fast.

name: Rocco's Tacos & Tequila Bar
address: 1313 E Las Olas Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301
description: The Las Olas staple for tacos and 400+ tequilas. The guac is made tableside, the margaritas are legendary, and the happy hour is one of the best deals on the strip. Loud, festive, and always packed.

name: Bokamper's Sports Bar & Grill
description: Fort Lauderdale's favorite sports bar with massive outdoor seating, live music on weekends, and a menu that goes way beyond bar food. Named after a Dolphins linebacker. The outdoor patio alone is worth the trip.

name: Tarpon Bend Food & Tackle
description: A downtown Fort Lauderdale fixture with a river-view patio, cold drafts, and a menu built for South Florida seafood lovers. Trivia nights, live music, and a Sunday brunch that locals swear by.

name: The Wilder
address: 611 N Andrews Ave, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33311
description: An Arts District hangout with rotating craft taps, a shaded backyard, and a lineup of local musicians and DJs who actually put in the work. Unpretentious, creative, and exactly the kind of bar Fort Lauderdale needed.
